sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, 
you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be 
repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the 
best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are the exams corrected yet?
(man)No, but they'll be corrected by noon.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'll correct the exams this afternoon.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it misinterprets the timeframe and the action; the man states the exams will be finished by noon, not that he will start correcting them in the afternoon.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyalahartikan kerangka waktu dan tindakan; pria tersebut menyatakan ujian akan selesai pada siang hari, bukan bahwa dia akan mulai mengoreksinya di sore hari.

options.text: The exam will be at noon.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it confuses the time of completion with the time the exam itself takes place.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena mencampuradukkan waktu penyelesaian dengan waktu pelaksanaan ujian itu sendiri.

options.text: He will collect the exams at 12:00.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it uses the word 'collect,' which is not synonymous with 'corrected' (graded).
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menggunakan kata 'collect' (mengumpulkan), yang bukan sinonim dari 'corrected' (dikoreksi/dinilai).

options.text: The tests will be graded by noon.

reason.en: This option is correct because it uses the synonym 'graded' for 'corrected' and accurately reflects the timeframe 'by noon' mentioned in the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena menggunakan sinonim 'graded' untuk 'corrected' dan secara akurat mencerminkan kerangka waktu 'by noon' yang disebutkan dalam dialog.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Has Martha's visa arrived yet?
(man)I think it arrived last month.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha applied for a visa last month.

reason.en: This option incorrectly assumes the action of applying happened last month, whereas the dialogue states the visa arrived.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengasumsikan tindakan melamar terjadi bulan lalu, padahal dialog menyatakan visa tersebut telah tiba.

options.text: Martha's visa will last for only a month.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the time reference 'last month' as the duration of the visa, which is not supported by the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan referensi waktu 'bulan lalu' sebagai durasi visa, yang tidak didukung oleh dialog.

options.text: Martha arrived last month without her visa.

reason.en: This option incorrectly suggests Martha herself arrived last month, while the subject of the dialogue is the visa.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menyarankan Martha sendiri yang tiba bulan lalu, padahal subjek dialog adalah visa tersebut.

options.text: One month ago Martha got her visa.

reason.en: This is the correct answer as it uses a synonym ('got' for 'arrived') to paraphrase the man's statement that the visa arrived last month.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena menggunakan sinonim ('got' untuk 'arrived') untuk memparafrase pernyataan pria tersebut bahwa visa tiba bulan lalu.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)What did the professor do in the first lass? I missed it because I was late.
(woman) She outlined the course requirements.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor described what the students should do.

reason.en: The woman states the professor 'outlined the course requirements.' This is synonymous with 'described what the students should do' in the context of a syllabus or course expectations.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan bahwa profesor 'menguraikan persyaratan kursus.' Ini sinonim dengan 'menjelaskan apa yang harus dilakukan siswa' dalam konteks silabus atau ekspektasi mata kuliah.

options.text: There was a long line to register for the required class.

reason.en: This option uses the word 'line' and 'register' to create a trap based on the word 'requirements' and 'class', which is irrelevant to the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata 'line' dan 'register' untuk membuat jebakan berdasarkan kata 'requirements' dan 'class', yang tidak relevan dengan dialog.

options.text: It is a requirement for each professor to teach at least one course.

reason.en: This option uses words from the dialogue ('requirement', 'professor', 'course') to create a confusing statement that does not reflect the meaning of the conversation.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata-kata dari dialog ('requirement', 'professor', 'course') untuk membuat pernyataan membingungkan yang tidak mencerminkan makna percakapan.

options.text: The professor required the class to prepare an outline.

reason.en: This option is a 'similar sound' or 'surface-level' trap, incorrectly using the word 'outline' as a verb for the students to perform, rather than the professor's action.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah jebakan 'bunyi serupa' atau 'tingkat permukaan', yang secara keliru menggunakan kata 'outline' sebagai kata kerja yang harus dilakukan siswa, bukan tindakan profesor.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) How did Chuck look when you visited him in the hospital?
(man)He's looked better.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Chuck had improved.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it suggests an improvement, which is the opposite of the man's intended meaning.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyiratkan adanya perbaikan kondisi, yang merupakan kebalikan dari maksud pria tersebut.

options.text: This visit was better than the last.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it misinterprets the comparison of the man's health with a previous visit.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena salah menafsirkan perbandingan kesehatan pria tersebut dengan kunjungan sebelumnya.

options.text: Chuck looked at him in the hospital.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it uses similar-sounding words ('looked') to create a trap, which is a common distractor in Part A.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menggunakan kata yang terdengar mirip ('looked') untuk membuat jebakan, yang merupakan pengecoh umum di Bagian A.

options.text: Chuck didn't seem to be doing very well.

reason.en: The man's statement 'He's looked better' is a negative expression implying that Chuck's current state is not good; therefore, he did not seem to be doing well.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria tersebut 'He's looked better' adalah ekspresi negatif yang menyiratkan bahwa kondisi Chuck saat ini tidak baik; oleh karena itu, dia tampak tidak sehat.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man)How much was tuition increased for next month?
(woman) More than I can afford.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She thinks the tuition should be raised.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman expresses concern about the cost, not an opinion on whether it should be raised.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena wanita tersebut mengungkapkan kekhawatiran tentang biaya, bukan opini apakah biaya tersebut harus dinaikkan.

options.text: The semesters tuition is quite affordable.

reason.en: This option contradicts the woman's statement that the tuition is 'more than I can afford'.
reason.id: Opsi ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut bahwa biaya kuliah 'lebih dari yang mampu ia bayar'.

options.text: She doesn't have enough money for her school fees.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The phrase 'more than I can afford' is a synonym for not having enough money to cover the required fees.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Frasa 'more than I can afford' adalah sinonim dari tidak memiliki cukup uang untuk menutupi biaya yang diperlukan.

options.text: She has more than enough for tuition.

reason.en: This option is the opposite of the woman's meaning, as she clearly states she cannot afford the increase.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah kebalikan dari maksud wanita tersebut, karena ia dengan jelas menyatakan bahwa ia tidak mampu membayar kenaikan tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) How were the grades on the history exam.
(man)No one got above a C.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks he got a good grade.

reason.en: This is incorrect because 'No one got above a C' implies that the grades were low, not good.
reason.id: Ini salah karena 'No one got above a C' menyiratkan bahwa nilai-nilainya rendah, bukan bagus.

options.text: The history grades were all C or above.

reason.en: This contradicts the dialogue, which states that no one achieved a grade higher than a C.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan dialog, yang menyatakan bahwa tidak ada seorang pun yang mencapai nilai lebih tinggi dari C.

options.text: No one got history grades.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the dialogue confirms that grades were given, but they were all C or lower.
reason.id: Ini salah karena dialog mengonfirmasi bahwa nilai diberikan, tetapi semuanya adalah C atau lebih rendah.

options.text: There were no high scores.

reason.en: This is correct because 'No one got above a C' is a negative expression meaning that all scores were C or lower, which is synonymous with having no high scores.
reason.id: Ini benar karena 'No one got above a C' adalah ekspresi negatif yang berarti semua nilai adalah C atau lebih rendah, yang sinonim dengan tidak adanya nilai tinggi.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) You know, this is the second time this week that you've been late to class!
(man)It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The parking lots were full before 10:00.

reason.en: The man states it was impossible to find a place to park, which is synonymous with the parking lots being full. This aligns with Skill 2 (synonyms) and Skill 1 (focus on the last line).
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa mustahil untuk menemukan tempat parkir, yang bersinonim dengan tempat parkir yang penuh. Ini sesuai dengan Skill 2 (sinonim) dan Skill 1 (fokus pada baris terakhir).

options.text: It was impossible to start class by 10:00.

reason.en: This is an incorrect interpretation; the man is explaining his lateness, not commenting on the ability to start the class itself.
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi yang salah; pria tersebut menjelaskan keterlambatannya, bukan mengomentari kemampuan untuk memulai kelas itu sendiri.

options.text: He parked the car before class at 10:00.

reason.en: This contradicts the dialogue, as the man explicitly states he could not find a place to park, meaning he did not park before the class.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan dialog, karena pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan dia tidak dapat menemukan tempat parkir, yang berarti dia tidak parkir sebelum kelas.

options.text: The possibility of finding a place to park increased.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man implies the opposite; the difficulty of finding a spot caused his lateness, not an increase in availability.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pria tersebut menyiratkan kebalikannya; kesulitan menemukan tempat parkir menyebabkan keterlambatannya, bukan peningkatan ketersediaan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man)Can I help you find something?
(woman) Yes, thank you. I need to get a new rug.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's found a new ring.

reason.en: This is a 'Similar Sound' trap. 'Ring' sounds like 'rug' but has a completely different meaning.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan 'Similar Sound'. 'Ring' terdengar mirip dengan 'rug' tetapi memiliki arti yang sangat berbeda.

options.text: She needs to help him find something.

reason.en: This misinterprets the dialogue; the woman is the one seeking help, not the one providing it.
reason.id: Ini salah mengartikan dialog; wanita tersebut adalah pihak yang mencari bantuan, bukan yang memberikan bantuan.

options.text: She's shopping for a carpet.

reason.en: Correct. 'Rug' is a synonym for 'carpet', and the context of the dialogue indicates she is shopping.
reason.id: Benar. 'Rug' adalah sinonim dari 'carpet', dan konteks dialog menunjukkan bahwa dia sedang berbelanja.

options.text: She's thankful she has a rag.

reason.en: This is a 'Similar Sound' trap. 'Rag' sounds like 'rug' and is used to confuse the listener.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan 'Similar Sound'. 'Rag' terdengar mirip dengan 'rug' dan digunakan untuk membingungkan pendengar.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) I'd like to open an account.
(man)Would you like a savings account or an interest-bearing checking account?
questions.question: 9. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSA TION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In a department store.

reason.en: A department store is a retail establishment, not a place where one opens financial accounts.
reason.id: Toko serba ada adalah tempat ritel, bukan tempat untuk membuka rekening keuangan.

options.text: In a bank.

reason.en: The mention of 'savings account' and 'interest-bearing checking account' are specific vocabulary cues that identify a bank as the setting.
reason.id: Penyebutan 'rekening tabungan' dan 'rekening giro berbunga' adalah petunjuk kosakata spesifik yang mengidentifikasi bank sebagai tempat kejadian.

options.text: In an accounting firm.

reason.en: An accounting firm deals with financial records and tax preparation, not the opening of personal bank accounts.
reason.id: Firma akuntansi berurusan dengan catatan keuangan dan persiapan pajak, bukan pembukaan rekening bank pribadi.

options.text: In a checkout line.

reason.en: A checkout line is where customers pay for goods, which is unrelated to the process of opening a new bank account.
reason.id: Antrean kasir adalah tempat pelanggan membayar barang, yang tidak berhubungan dengan proses pembukaan rekening bank baru.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
(woman) Why does Jane spend so much time in San Francisco?
(man)She has a cousin there, so she likes to visit, especially during the holidays.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Jane usually visits San Francisco for her vacations.

reason.en: The man states that Jane visits her cousin in San Francisco, especially during the holidays. This paraphrases to Jane usually visiting San Francisco for her vacations.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa Jane mengunjungi sepupunya di San Francisco, terutama saat liburan. Ini merupakan parafrasa dari Jane biasanya mengunjungi San Francisco untuk liburannya.

options.text: Jane's cousin often visits San Francisco.

reason.en: This option incorrectly identifies the cousin as the one visiting San Francisco, whereas the dialogue states Jane is the one visiting.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengidentifikasi sepupu sebagai orang yang mengunjungi San Francisco, padahal dialog menyatakan Jane-lah yang berkunjung.

options.text: Whenever there's a holiday, Jane's cousin goes to San Francisco.

reason.en: This option incorrectly suggests the cousin is the one traveling to San Francisco during holidays, which contradicts the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menyarankan sepupunya yang bepergian ke San Francisco saat liburan, yang bertentangan dengan dialog.

options.text: Whenever there's a holiday, Jane leaves San Francisco.

reason.en: This option incorrectly suggests Jane leaves San Francisco during holidays, while the dialogue states she visits during those times.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menyarankan Jane meninggalkan San Francisco saat liburan, padahal dialog menyatakan dia berkunjung pada waktu tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you really hungry?
(man)I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks.
questions.question: 11.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'd really like to have something to eat.

reason.en: The man uses the idiomatic expression 'I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks' to emphasize that he is extremely hungry. Option 0 correctly paraphrases this as a desire to have something to eat.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menggunakan ungkapan idiomatik 'I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks' untuk menekankan bahwa dia sangat lapar. Opsi 0 secara tepat memparafrasekan ini sebagai keinginan untuk makan sesuatu.

options.text: Because he's weak, he can't eat.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the idiom literally, assuming physical weakness prevents him from eating, which is not the intended meaning.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menafsirkan idiom tersebut secara harfiah, dengan asumsi kelemahan fisik mencegahnya makan, yang bukan merupakan makna yang dimaksudkan.

options.text: It's been weeks since he's had anything to eat.

reason.en: This is a trap answer that takes the speaker's hyperbolic statement literally. The man is not stating a factual duration of time, but expressing the intensity of his hunger.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban jebakan yang menafsirkan pernyataan hiperbolis pembicara secara harfiah. Pria tersebut tidak menyatakan durasi waktu yang faktual, melainkan mengungkapkan intensitas rasa laparnya.

options.text: He hasn't felt like eating for weeks.

reason.en: This option contradicts the meaning of the dialogue, as the man is expressing a strong desire to eat, not a lack of appetite.
reason.id: Opsi ini bertentangan dengan makna dialog, karena pria tersebut mengungkapkan keinginan kuat untuk makan, bukan kurangnya nafsu makan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 17: Listen for Idioms

questions.passage:
(man)The traffic outside is really loud!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Traffic should not be allowed.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it interprets the dialogue literally and adds an opinion not expressed by the speakers.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menafsirkan dialog secara harfiah dan menambahkan opini yang tidak diungkapkan oleh pembicara.

options.text: She thinks that the traffic should stay Outside.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it misinterprets the idiom 'I'll say' as a literal statement about location.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyalahartikan idiom 'I'll say' sebagai pernyataan harfiah tentang lokasi.

options.text: She agrees that the traffic is noisy.

reason.en: This is correct. The expression 'I'll say!' is a common idiom used to express strong agreement with the previous speaker's statement.
reason.id: Ini benar. Ekspresi 'I'll say!' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk menyatakan persetujuan kuat terhadap pernyataan pembicara sebelumnya.

options.text: She'll stay outside with the man.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it confuses the word 'outside' in the dialogue with a physical action of staying there.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena mengacaukan kata 'outside' dalam dialog dengan tindakan fisik untuk tetap berada di sana.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you seen the headlines yet
today?
I haven't had a chance to read a word.
questions.question: 13.WHAT ARE THEY PROBABLY DIS CUSSING?

options.text: The headings for today's reading assignment.

reason.en: This option is a trap using the word 'headings' which sounds similar to 'headlines' but refers to a different context.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah jebakan yang menggunakan kata 'headings' yang terdengar mirip dengan 'headlines' namun merujuk pada konteks yang berbeda.

options.text: The chance to make the headlines.

reason.en: This option uses the word 'headlines' from the dialogue but creates a nonsensical meaning regarding the chance to make them.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata 'headlines' dari dialog namun menciptakan makna yang tidak masuk akal mengenai kesempatan untuk membuatnya.

options.text: Her reading ability.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the phrase 'read a word' as a literal comment on her ability to read, rather than an idiom for not having read anything yet.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan frasa 'read a word' sebagai komentar harfiah tentang kemampuan membacanya, alih-alih sebagai idiom yang berarti belum membaca apa pun.

options.text: The daily newspaper.

reason.en: The term 'headlines' is a direct reference to the news, and the context of reading them today logically points to the daily newspaper.
reason.id: Istilah 'headlines' adalah rujukan langsung ke berita, dan konteks membacanya hari ini secara logis merujuk pada surat kabar harian.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
(man)I'm not ready yet, and it's going to take me a while longer.
(woman) You'd better hurry. Take five minutes too long, and you'll miss the bus.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The bus trip is only five minutes long.

reason.en: [please evaluate-manual] Based on the passage, this option is factually incorrect as it misinterprets the woman's warning about time as the duration of the bus trip, but it is marked as the correct index.
reason.id: [please evaluate-manual] Berdasarkan teks, opsi ini secara faktual salah karena menyalahartikan peringatan wanita tersebut tentang waktu sebagai durasi perjalanan bus, namun ini ditandai sebagai index yang benar.

options.text: The man missed the bus by five minutes.

reason.en: The dialogue is in the present tense regarding a future possibility; the man has not missed the bus yet.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut menggunakan bentuk waktu sekarang mengenai kemungkinan di masa depan; pria tersebut belum ketinggalan bus.

options.text: The man should hurry to catch the bus.

reason.en: The woman explicitly tells the man 'You'd better hurry' to avoid missing the bus, which is the logical inference of the dialogue.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit mengatakan kepada pria itu 'You'd better hurry' agar tidak ketinggalan bus, yang merupakan kesimpulan logis dari dialog tersebut.

options.text: The bus was five minutes late.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the bus being late; it discusses the man's potential to miss the bus due to his own delay.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan bus terlambat; dialog tersebut membahas potensi pria itu ketinggalan bus karena keterlambatannya sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)I think it's impossible for me to pass this class.
(woman) You should never say "impossible."
questions.question: 15. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: It's not possible to pass the class.

reason.en: This option repeats the man's negative sentiment and contradicts the woman's advice to avoid the word 'impossible'.
reason.id: Opsi ini mengulangi sentimen negatif pria tersebut dan bertentangan dengan saran wanita untuk menghindari kata 'mustahil'.

options.text: She'll definitely fail.

reason.en: This is a misinterpretation of the woman's encouragement; she is not predicting failure, but rather challenging the man's defeatist attitude.
reason.id: Ini adalah salah tafsir dari dorongan wanita tersebut; dia tidak memprediksi kegagalan, melainkan menantang sikap pesimis pria itu.

options.text: It's always possible.

reason.en: The woman advises against using the word 'impossible', which implies that there is always a possibility of success.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyarankan untuk tidak menggunakan kata 'mustahil', yang menyiratkan bahwa selalu ada kemungkinan untuk berhasil.

options.text: She shouldn't say anything about the class.

reason.en: This is a literal and incorrect interpretation of the woman's advice; she is telling him not to label the situation as impossible, not to remain silent.
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi harfiah yang salah dari saran wanita tersebut; dia menyuruh pria itu untuk tidak melabeli situasi sebagai mustahil, bukan untuk tetap diam.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(Man)  Why were you thanking Tom?
(Man)  He lent me enough money to pay the rent.
questions.question: 16.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She gave Tom money to pay the rent.

reason.en: This option incorrectly identifies the woman as the lender, whereas the dialogue states Tom lent the money to her.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengidentifikasi wanita tersebut sebagai pemberi pinjaman, padahal dialog menyatakan Tom yang meminjamkan uang kepadanya.

options.text: She was given money for the rent.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The dialogue uses an active voice ('He lent me'), which is correctly paraphrased in the passive voice ('She was given money').
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Dialog menggunakan kalimat aktif ('He lent me'), yang diparafrasekan dengan benar ke dalam kalimat pasif ('She was given money').

options.text: Tom borrowed money for the rent.

reason.en: This option incorrectly suggests Tom is the borrower, while the dialogue confirms he is the lender.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menyarankan Tom adalah peminjam, sementara dialog mengonfirmasi bahwa dia adalah pemberi pinjaman.

options.text: She had some money to lend.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman is the one who needed the money, not the one who had money to lend.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena wanita tersebut adalah pihak yang membutuhkan uang, bukan pihak yang memiliki uang untuk dipinjamkan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 5: Listen for Who and What in Passives

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you enjoying the dessert?
(man)Never have I tasted such delicious  cake!
questions.question: 17.WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE CAKE?

options.text: The cake is extremely good.

reason.en: The man uses an emphatic inversion 'Never have I tasted such delicious cake,' which is a superlative way of saying the cake is the best he has ever had, meaning it is extremely good.

parts.title: Part B
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear longer conversations. After each conversation, you will hear several questions. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. 

After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

parts.title: Part C
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear several talks. After each talk, you will hear some 
questions. The talks and questions will not be repeated. 
After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best 
answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Here is an example. 
On the recording, you hear: 
(narrator) Listen to an instructor talk to his class about painting. 
(man) Artist Grant Wood was a guiding force in the school'of painting known as American 
regionalist, a style reflecting the distinctive characteristics of art from rural areas of 
the United States. Wood began drawing animals on the family farm at the age of three, 
and when he was thirty-eight one of his paintings received a remarkable amount of 
. public notice and acclaim. This painting, called "American Gothic," is a starkly 
simple depiction of a serious couple staring directly out at the viewer. 

Now listen to a sample question.
(narrator) What style of painting is known as American regionalist?

In your test book, you read:
(A) Art from America s inner cities
(B) Art from the central region of the United States 
(C) Art from various urban areas in the United States 
(D) Art from rural sections of America 

The best answer to the question "What style of painting is known as American regionalist?" is (D), "Art from rural sections of America." Therefore, the correct choice is (D). 

Now listen to another sample question.
(narrator) What is the name of Wood's most successful painting? 
In your test book, you read:
(A) "American Regionalist"
(B) "The Family Farm in Iowa"
(C) "American Gothic" 
(D) "A Serious Couple" 

The best answer to the question, "What is the name of Wood's most successful painting?" is (C), "American Gothic." Therefore, the correct choice is (C). 

Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

Questions 43-46
(narrator) Listen to an instructor talk to her class about Walt Whitman. 
(woman) The topic of today's lecture is Walt Whitman, an American poet and author of the renowned collection of poems Leaves of Grass. This volume of poems is a celebration of America, full of pride in the United States and reverence for the goals of American democracy. 

Whitman began writing Leaves of Grass in the middle of the nineteenth century, and the first edition appeared in 1855 with only twelve poems. Several other editions of Leaves of Grass appeared throughout Whitman's lifetime with additional poems; Leaves of Grass grew and matured right along with Whitman. 

The longest and best-known poem in Leaves of Grass is "Song of Myself," which appeared in the first edition. The poem "When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed" was added to a later edition. This poem was written at the time of Abraham Lincoln's death in 1865 and contained Whitman's reflections on that event. Lincoln's death occurred in April, in the spring, in a season of new life. This poem reflects that spring can be at the same time a period of death and a period of rebirth.
